code.description: 0 indicates no error, any other code is an error code
0 indicates no error, any other code is an error code

Reports.description:
Root XML wrapper for FortiSIEM event query requests.

Two request styles are supported:

**1. Standard Report XML**
- Uses structured report elements such as `SelectClause`, `PatternClause`, and `ReportInterval`
- Best when you want to reuse FortiSIEM report exports with minor edits

**2. SQL Advanced XML**
- Uses `ClickHouseSQL/SQL`
- Available for ClickHouse-backed deployments
- Best when you want full control of the query text

For Standard Report XML, `ReportInterval/Low` and `High` are Unix epoch seconds.

Description.description: Optional descriptive text for the report.

SelectClause.description:
Standard Report select list.

`AttrList` contains the returned columns and may include aggregate functions such as:
- `COUNT(*)`
- `SUM(sentBytes64)`
- `SUM(recvBytes64)`

numEntries.description: Optional XML attribute controlling result entry count.

AttrList.description: Comma-separated list of selected attributes or aggregate expressions.

OrderByClause.description: Optional ordering for Standard Report queries.

PatternClause.description:
Standard Report filter section.

At least one `SubPattern` is required for Standard Report queries.

window.description: Optional XML attribute specifying pattern window in seconds.

SubPattern.description:
A single FortiSIEM filter block.

`SingleEvtConstr` behaves like a FortiSIEM-specific WHERE clause.

name.description: Required logical name for the subpattern.

GroupByAttr.description:
Optional comma-separated group-by list.

Required when aggregate functions are used in `SelectClause`, and grouped fields should also appear in `SelectClause/AttrList`.

ReportInterval.description:
Standard Report query time range.

Values are Unix epoch **seconds**.

Low.description: Inclusive start time in Unix epoch seconds.

High.description: Inclusive end time in Unix epoch seconds.

ClickHouseSQL.description:
SQL Advanced query block for ClickHouse-backed FortiSIEM deployments.

It is strongly recommended to filter on `phRecvTime` for performance.

SQL.description: Raw ClickHouse SQL query text, commonly wrapped in CDATA.
SQL.example:
SELECT
  reptDevIpAddrV4 AS reptIp,
  eventType,
  any(rawEventMsg) AS rawEventMsg,
  max(phRecvTime) AS last_seen
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE phRecvTime >= fromUnixTimestamp(1775579461)
  AND phRecvTime <= fromUnixTimestamp(1775665861)
GROUP BY ALL
ORDER BY reptIp

EventDataResults.description:
XML response wrapper for paginated event query results.

Result rows can appear in two common forms:
- `attributes.attribute[]` for Standard Report queries
- `dataStr.entry[]` for SQL Advanced queries

code.description: 0 indicates success; non-zero values indicate an error.

eventType.description: Present in many SQL Advanced result rows.

EventDataStr.description: SQL Advanced result payload represented as key/value entries.

EventAttributes.description: Standard Report result payload represented as named attributes.

incidentId.description: This is the unique incident ID for this individual trigger of this incident.

incidentTitle.example: Scanner found severe vulnerability Microsoft Windows Pragmatic General Multicast (PGM) Remote Code Execution Vulnerability on lab01

incidentFirstSeen.description: Incident First Seen time in epoch milliseconds

incidentLastSeen.description: Incident Last Seen time in epoch milliseconds

incidentReso.description:
Incident Resolution integer value

None = 0 Open = 1 TruePositive = 2 FalsePositive = 3 InProgress = 4

incidentRptIp.description: Incident Reporting IP - Log source of Incident

incidentRptDevName.description: This is the Incident Reporting Device Name - The hostname of the log source of the incident.

incidentSrc.description: Attributes identifying the source of the incident, defined as a comma separated list of <attribute>:<value> pairs. This is optional and depends on the incident.

incidentTarget.description: Attributes identifying the target of the incident, defined as a comma separated list of <attribute>:<value> pairs. This is optional and depends on the incident.

count.description: Incident Count. While the incident is active, this is the number of times the ACTIVE incident has triggered for a distinct event window.

attackTechnique.example: [{\"name\": \"Endpoint Denial of Service: Application or System Exploitation\", \"techniqueid\": \"T1499.004\"}]

attackTactic.description: This is the Mitre tactic associated with the incident

phSubIncidentCategory.description: Incident Subcategory, often synonymous with Mitre tactic but can be other categories as well.

eventType.description: This is a incident event type, a special unique identifier of the incident/rule, not to be confused with a raw event event type.

eventName.description: This is the incident name (display name), equivalent to the underlying rule name

incidentClearedTime.description: Incident cleared time in epoch milliseconds, if 0, it has never been cleared

incidentDetail.example: vulnName:Microsoft Windows Pragmatic General Multicast (PGM) Remote Code Execution Vulnerability,
incidentDetail.description: Incident detail metadata associated with the incident, defined as a comma separated list of <attribute>:<value> pairs. This is optional and depends on the incident

incidentStatus.description:
'integer incident status integer types to filter by:

ACTIVE = 0
AUTOMATICALLY CLEARED = 1
MANUALLY CLEARED = 2
SYSTEM CLEARED = 3
'

phCustId.description: This is the FortiSIEM Organization ID

incidentExtTicketId.description: This is the ticket ID as defined in the external system.

incidentExtTicketState.description: This is the state of the case as defined in external system

comments.example: SOC team investigated and determined the source IP indicator x.x.x.x to be malicous

total.description: total number of incidents matched in your query

pages.description: indicates total number of pages to paginate over, includes the first page.

start.description: This is the starting object of the range in this page of events. The first api call will state 0

sizePerPage.description: Number of incidents returned per page of the query

queryId.description: This is the query id to use for paginating the rest of the requests if more than one page.

custId.description: The FortiSIEM organization ID for which the event belongs

index.description: The integer index of the event, in the order it was returned to the client. Not needed by client in most cases

id.description: This is the raw event unique ID. You can query this in analytics by using Event ID = 4895412795018307522

eventType.description: The event type for the event.

receiveTime.description: event receive time in epoch milliseconds

rawMessage.description: This is the original raw unparsed event

attributes.description: These are the parsed attributes from the event

result.description: Result object showing status code and message

code.description: Code 0 means success, anything else failure

description.example: 1 event found in this time range

queryId.description: Returns the query ID, which is used to query the progress of the triggering event

pub_get_trigger_event_progress_resp.description: Returns the progress in percentage for the query id provided in the request

pub_get_incident_post_payload.description: Payload for use in customizing search filters, fields, and orderBy clause

timeTo.description: Required if incidentId filter list is not specified

timeFrom.description: Required if incidentId filter list is not specified

start.description: optional offset to start with, not needed if you are iterating using queryId and page numbers

size.description: Number of items to return per page of incidents

orderBy.description: You can set the descending or ascending order of searched incidents using any datetime attribute such as incidentLastSeen. This is optional

descending.description: Specify if sort order is ascending or descending. Only needed if orderBy set

items.description: Array of all the incident fields you would liked to return

id.description: id identifying an individual watch list entry

triggeringRules.example: Sudden Change In DNS Data Transfer Pattern From A Specific Host

entryValue.description: This is the value of the individual entry, depending on the watchlist its value will have a different meaning. E.G. a watchlist of suspicious users, the value would be the username

ageOut.description: Lists the expiry time of the entry in human readable format

count.description: This is a count of how many incidents have triggered, that have a watchlist rule that would have populated with this entry. E.g. we have a watchlist entry for user1, if 100 incidents have user1, the count will reflect here.

If the agent is indeed sending events successfully, ensure the agent is the latest supported version for your version of FortiSIEM

True means Worker upload queue is less than 100MB and Worker will accept events.

False means Worker upload queue is more than 100MB and Worker will reject events. This is likely because inserts to event database is slow.

accessIp.description: This was the IP used to discover the device. Either this was the log source of a packet sent by the device or the IP used to poll the device in a discovery

naturalId.description: This is the internal name used to represent the device, often not needed by user

approved.description: This represents if the device was marked approved instead of pending or unmanaged. If a device is neither approved or unmanaged, it is in state pending. Pending and approved both consume a device license, the distinguisher is helpful for soc teams to review newly added devices.

unmanaged.description: If a device is unmanaged, it does not consume a FortiSIEM device license, and all logs are dropped, no analysis will occur on the device.

filter.description:
Supported filter fields:
id, name, accessIp, deviceType.vendor, deviceType.model, status,
discoverMethod, policyName, deviceStatus.agentStatus, agentType,
agentVersion, description, deviceStatus.eventStatus, location,
deviceStatus.monitorStatus, eventParserList.name, purdueLevel, deviceTag

status.description: Device status code. Response may return the string label.

fields.description:
List of fields to return.

Notes:
  - If `fields` is specified, `id` must be included.
  - `customProperties` returns all custom properties.
  - If `filter` is used, clients should also provide `fields`.

select.description: Comma-separated select list. Supports aliases such as `COUNT(*) "count"`.

EventQueryV2CompleteRequest.description: Full request object including all optional fields.

from.description: Start of query range in Unix epoch seconds.

to.description: End of query range in Unix epoch seconds.

EventQueryV2CustomerScope.description: Optional customer scoping rules for Standard Report queries.

all.description: When true, all customers are included.

sql.example: SELECT reptDevIpAddrV4 AS reptIp, eventType, any(rawEventMsg) AS rawEventMsg, max(phRecvTime) AS last_seen FROM fsiem.events WHERE phRecvTime > (now() - 3600) GROUP BY ALL ORDER BY reptIp

ctes.description: Optional CTE definitions or supporting SQL text.

EventQueryV2StandardRow.description: Example shape for Standard Report result rows. Actual keys depend on the submitted select list.

EventQueryV2AdvancedRow.description: Example shape for SQL Advanced result rows. Actual keys depend on the SQL select list.

custProperties.description: Optional custom properties container for the organization.

organization.description: Org to which te device belongs

domains.description: One or more orgs are listed

disabled.description: Checks if the org is disabled

initialized.description: Checks if the org is initialized

post.summary: List CMDB Devices and their properties
post.description:
This API is used to list and display CMDB devices. **Pagination:** Pagination is offset and limit based. So start=0 for first object in response, and size defaults to 50 objects per API call (0–49). The response returns a "total" field indicating the total number of objects. If total exceeds the page size, the client must increment `start` by `size` until all objects are retrieved. **Examples**
A barebones example with no query filter and no field filter: curl -X POST https://fortisiem.example.com/phoenix/rest/pub/device \ -u 'super/admin:password' \ -H "Content-Type: application/json" \ -d '{ "start": 0, "size": 50 }' **NOTE**: Filtering by `custId` (FortiSIEM tenantID) is optional for MSSP deployments. If not specified devices from all tenants are returned. A small example using filter and field display modifiers - give me all CMDB devices that are status Pending and the model is FortiOS: curl -k -X POST https://fortisiem.example.com/phoenix/rest/pub/device \ -u 'super/admin:password' \ -H "Content-Type: application/json" \ -d '{ "start": 0, "size": 20, "filter": { "status": "Pending", "deviceType.model": "FortiOS" }, "fields": [ "id", "name", "accessIp", "status", "discoverMethod", "riskScore", "lastModifiedTime" ] }' NOTES: 1) If you filter by status, you must specify the integer status 0,1,2,3 instead of string, response gives the string value 2) If filter is provided in query, but no field selections - default fields will not populate. You must always use fields and filters together 3) When selecting fields, id field must be specified along with desired fields

requestBody.description: A query request based on optional filters and set of fields to return for each matching CMDB device

filterByCustIdNoFields.summary: Filter by custId only (default fields returned)
filterByCustIdNoFields.description: Returns devices for a specific organization using default field set.

filterByStatusWithFields.summary: Filter by status with explicit fields
filterByStatusWithFields.description:
Returns only selected fields for devices with a specific status.
Note: status must be provided as an integer.

filterByVendorAndModel.description: Example filtering on nested deviceType fields.

minimalRequest.description: Returns first page of devices using default behavior.

get.summary: List monitored devices in CMDB (shown in Admin > Setup > Monitor Performance page)
get.description: Displays a short description (deviceName,accessIp,deviceType,Organization,Monitors) of the monitored devices

200.description: OK. An XML containing device name, device type, organization name and list of monitored attributes is returned

post.summary: Delete one or more CMDB devices by specifying device IDs
post.description: When the request succeeds (HTTP response code 200), a JSON object is returned. It contains the list of device ids that were successfully deleted and the list of device ids for which delete failed

200.description: OK. Sucess and Failed deviceIds are returned

requestBody.description: The request body should be a JSON with a list of device ids. the list of device ids can be obtained via the Get CMDB Device List API
